libraryDescription:
This library, gui.TextFieldWindow, provides a window containing arranged text fields as a GUI component.

In the standard VCSSL library GUI, GUI components are handled as int IDs, but the GUI component provided by this module is handled as theTextFieldWindow struct.

The TextFieldWindow struct internally stores resource IDs and related data, but you normally do not need to be aware of its internals becausefunctions for creating and destroying it are provided. Direct access to its members is not recommended.
Specifically, create it with the newTextFieldWindow function and destroy it with thedeleteComponent function.

This module also provides overloads of standard GUI library functions so that the TextFieldWindow struct can be handled almost in the sameway as in the standard library GUI.
For example, the mountComponent, setComponentSize, andsetComponentLocation functions are provided.

function0.name: newTextFieldWindow
function0.declaration: TextFieldWindow newTextFieldWindow(int x, int y, int width, int height, string title, string labelText[ ], string defaultValue[ ])
function0.description: Creates a text field window with its size determined automatically.
function0.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window. 
(string type) title : The title of the window. 
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text of all text fields. 
(string[] type) defaultValue : The array storing the default values of all text fields.

function1.name: newTextFieldWindow
function1.description: Creates a text field window with its size determined automatically.
function1.parameters:
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text of all text fields. 
defaultText : The array storing the default values of all text fields.

function2.name: deleteComponent
function2.parameters: (TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The window to be destroyed.

function3.name: setTextLabelWidth
function3.description: Sets the width of the label area.
function3.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target text field window. 
(int type) labelWidth : The width of the label area.

function4.name: setButton
function4.description: Displays a button at the bottom of the window.
function4.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(string type) label : The label text of the button.

function5.name: getButton
function5.description: Gets the GUI component ID of the button.
function5.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the button.

function6.name: getWindow
function6.description: Gets the GUI component ID of the window.
function6.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the window.

function7.name: getTextField
function7.description: Gets the text field corresponding to the specified label.
function7.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(string type) labelText : The label text corresponding to the check box to get.
function7.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function8.name: getTextField
function8.description: Gets the text field at the specified index.
function8.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) index : The index of the check box.
function8.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function9.name: getComponentSize
function9.description: Returns the size of the window.
function9.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the size of the window.

function10.name: setComponentSize
function10.declaration: void setComponentSize(TextFieldWindow &windowInstance, int width, int height)
function10.description: Sets the size of the window.
function10.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) width : The height.
function10.returnValue: (void type) The array storing the size of the window.

function11.name: setComponentSize
function11.description: Automatically adjusts the sizes of the internal components to match the current window size. Use this when the window size has been changed externally.

function12.name: getComponentLocation
function12.description: Returns the location of the window.
function12.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the window.

function13.name: getComponentLocation
function13.description: Returns the location of the window.
function13.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the window.

function14.name: setComponentLocation
function14.declaration: void setComponentLocation(TextFieldWindow &windowInstance, int x, int y)
function14.description: Sets the location of the window.
function14.parameters:
(TextFieldWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the window.
